shortDescription.text: Identifies content that is purely descriptive, instructional, and poses no security threat.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags content that has been verified to be non-executable, non-malicious, and aligns with legitimate software development or operational practices. It confirms the absence of malicious intent, exploit primitives, or dangerous security practices.

message.text: The skill content, described as 'testing-best-practices', has been verified as purely descriptive and instructional, outlining guidelines for software testing. Threat intelligence (e.g., https://github.com/nadvolod/testing-best-practices, gorillalogic.com articles) strongly CONFIRMS the legitimacy of this topic and its common association with non-malicious development practices. No executable code, shell commands, network requests, file system operations, or any other behavior that could be interpreted as malicious or suspicious was identified. No confirmed malicious intent.
